name: Oyster Artichoke Bisque
recipeIngredient: 6 small Artichokes (cook and scrape, chop bottoms)
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt the butter in a 5-quart Dutch oven.
2. Saute onions, celery, and bell pepper until they soften.
3. Add bacon drippings.
4. Stir in chopped oysters and simmer gently over low heat, stirring constantly, until oysters curl and a rich gray-colored base forms.
5. Drop in artichoke pulp and bottoms; blend them well into the mixture.
6. Stir in oyster liquor and artichoke cooking stock.
7. Bring to a boil, ensuring constant stirring to prevent scorching or sticking.
8. Boil for about 4 minutes.
9. Stir in basil, reduce heat to simmer, and let cook slowly for another 5 minutes.
10. Stir in whipping cream gradually until the mixture returns to a boil.
11. Reduce heat so it barely bubbles, then add salt and pepper (taste before adding).
12. After 10 minutes, remove from heat; allow it to set up for 15 minutes.
13. Serve garnished with parsley.
